title: Spreading rash and mucosal ulcers in a 28-year-old
presentation: A 28-year-old male started on allopurinol 3 weeks ago presents with 4 days of fever, sore throat, and a rapidly spreading painful rash starting on the trunk. He now has blistering on his lips, painful eye redness, and genital ulcers. Vitals: BP 118/76, HR 108, Temp 39.2C, RR 18, SpO2 97%. Exam: widespread erythematous macules with central blistering, positive Nikolsky sign, extensive oral and genital mucosal erosions, bilateral conjunctival injection.

gold_standard_workup.key_decision_point: Body surface area <10% = SJS, >30% = TEN. Positive Nikolsky sign confirms diagnosis. STOP the offending drug immediately — this is the most critical intervention. Ophthalmology urgently — eye involvement can cause permanent blindness. Burns unit management.
